sections.paragraph:
τελαμών (τελᾰμών, ῶνος,)

1. a broad strap for bearing anything


Etym. from Root ΤΛΑ, *τλάω, whence also the hero Telamon took his name, cf. Ἄτλας

1. a leathern strap or belt, for bearing both shield or sword, Hom.

2. a broad linen bandage for wounds, Il., Hdt., Eur.; for swathing mummies, Hdt.
